This document is a publication of the French Patent & Trademark Office (Institut National de la Propriété Industrielle,

INPI – Supplementary protection certificates – Guidelines for patent and utility certificate applications – December 2023
2 / INPI / SUPPLEMENTARY PROTECTION CERTIFICATES - SPC / Last change to this page: December 2023

SUPPLEMENTARY PROTECTION CERTIFICATE
The supplementary protection certificate (hereinafter “SPC”) extends the duration
of the protection of a product (active ingredient) found in the composition of a
medicinal or plant protection product covered by a patent that protects a product,
a process to obtain a product or an application of a product.
The conditions governing the grant and validity of certificates for medicinal
products are set out in Regulation (EC) No. 469/2009 of the European Parliament
and of the Council of 6 May 2009 (Official Journal of the European Union [“OJ”] L
152/1 of 16 June 2009), codifying Council Regulation (EEC) No 1768/92 of 18 June
1992, which entered into force on 2 January 1993 and was amended in particular
by Regulation (EC) No. 1901/2006 of the European Parliament and of the Council
of 12 December 2006, which entered entry into force on 26 January 2007.
The conditions governing the grant and validity of certificates for plant protection
products are set out in Regulation (EC) 1610/96 of the European Parliament and
of the Council of 23 July 1996 (OJ L 198/31 of 8 August 1996). These provisions
entered into force on 8 February 1997.
Last paragraph These two regulations contain identical provisions with regard to the conditions
of art. L.611-2 of governing the grant of a certificate. Where aspects of the application procedure are
the French not dealt with, the provisions applicable under national law with regard to patent
Intellectual applications shall apply.
Property Code
(hereinafter
“IPC”)
Art. R.617-2 of
the IPC
Art.19-1 of
Reg. 469/2009
1. OBTAINING AN SPC: SUBSTANTIVE CONDITIONS
Art. 3 of “A certificate shall be granted if, in the Member State [France] in which
Reg. 469/2009 the application referred to in Article 7 is submitted and at the date of that
Reg. 1610/96 application:
(a) the product is protected by a basic patent in force;
(b) a valid authorisation to place the product on the market as a medicinal
product has been granted in accordance with Directive 2001/83/EC or
Directive 2001/82/EC, as appropriate;
(c) the product has not already been the subject of a certificate;
(d) the authorisation referred to in (b) is the first authorisation to place
the product on the market as a medicinal product.”
Art. 7 of
Reg. 469/2009 The application for a certificate shall be lodged within a set time limit (see
Reg. 1610/96 paragraph e below).
3 / INPI / SUPPLEMENTARY PROTECTION CERTIFICATES - SPC / Last change to this page: December 2023

a) Definition of “product”
Art. 1 (a and  In the context of a “medicinal product”, “product” shall mean the
b) of Reg. “active ingredient or combination of active ingredients of a medicinal
469/2009 product”.
A “medicinal product” shall mean “any substance or combination of
substances presented for treating or preventing disease in human
beings or animals and any substance or combination of substances
which may be administered to human beings or animals with a view to
making a medical diagnosis or to restoring, correcting or modifying
physiological functions in humans or in animals”;
ECJ Santen The definition of “product” does not take into account the therapeutic
C-673/18 use of the active ingredient, or the combination of active ingredients
09/07/2020 protected by said patent.
 In the context of “plant protection products”, “product” shall mean
the “active substance” or the “combination of active substances of a
plant protection product”.
Active substances are defined as “substances or micro-organisms
Art.1 of including viruses, having general or specific action:
Reg.1610/96 (a) against harmful organisms;
or
(b) on plants, parts of plants or plant products”.
“Plant protection products” shall mean “active substances and
preparations containing one or more active substances, put up in the
form in which they are supplied to the user, intended to:
(a) protect plants or plant products against all harmful organisms or
prevent the action of such organisms, in so far as such substances or
preparations are not otherwise defined below;
(b) influence the life processes of plants, other than as a nutrient (e.g.
plant growth regulators);
(c) preserve plant products, in so far as such substances or products are
not subject to special Council or Commission provisions on preservatives;
(d) destroy undesirable plants;
or
(e) destroy parts of plants, check or prevent undesirable growth of plants;
b) The basic patent
1. Origin of the basic patent
Art. 2 and 3(a) A basic patent shall mean a patent having effect in France that is in force
Reg. 469/2009 on the filing date of the application for an SPC and still has effect on the
Art. 2 and 3 examination date of the application for the certificate or has reached its
(1.a) Reg. legal expiry date after the filing date of the SPC. It can therefore be a
1610/96 French or European patent having effect in France.
2. Subject-matter of the basic patent
 With respect to medicinal products:
Art. 1 (c) of - The basic patent is a patent that protects the product as such, a process
Reg. 469/2009 to obtain the product or an application of the product.
ECJ Yissum - The application of the product may concern a second medical use if the
Research
first application has not already given rise to the grant of an SPC.
C-202-05
17/04/2007
4 / INPI / SUPPLEMENTARY PROTECTION CERTIFICATES - SPC / Last change to this page: December 2023

Art. 1 (9) of  With respect to plant protection products:
Reg. 1610/96 The basic patent is a patent that protects the product as such, a
preparation containing the product, a process to obtain the product or
an application of the product.
 Common terms and conditions:
Art.2 and 3(a) Whether it concerns medicinal products or plant protection products, the
Reg.469/2009 product must be covered by the claims of the basic patent. Indeed, given
Reg. 1610/96 that the scope of protection of the patent is defined by these claims, it is
not sufficient for the product that is subject to the SPC application to
merely be described in a working example of the invention without being
covered by the claims.
ECJ Yeda If a patent claims that a product is composed of two active ingredients
C-518/10 but does not make any claim in relation to one of those active ingredients
25/11/2011 individually, an SPC cannot be granted on the basis of such a patent for
the one active ingredient considered in isolation.
ECJ Eli Lilly
C-493/12
12/12/2013 An active ingredient which satisfies a general functional definition in the
ECJ Teva claims of a patent, without being singled out as a specific embodiment
C-121/17 to be drawn from the teaching of said patent, can be regarded as
25/07/18 protected by the patent, on condition that, based on those claims,
Royalty interpreted inter alia in the light of the description of the invention, a
Pharma person skilled in the art is able to conclude from their general knowledge
C-650/17 of the field in question at the filing or priority date of the basic patent,
30/04/2020 and from the state of the art at this same date, that the claims relate,
Court of implicitly but necessarily and specifically, to the active ingredient in
Cassation question.
Plenary
session
01/02/2023
Ono and
Wyeth
ECJ M.I.T. If the medicinal product is composed of an active substance and a
C-431/04 second substance that has no therapeutic effect of its own but is used to
04/05/06 obtain a pharmaceutical form of the medicinal product necessary for the
therapeutic efficacy of the first substance, only the first substance shall
be deemed to be the product. The second substance shall be deemed to
be an excipient.
Court of
Cassation When the marketing authorisation does not qualify a substance as an
Plenary “active ingredient”, it is understood as a rebuttable presumption that this
session substance does not produce a pharmacological, immunological, or
01/02/2023 metabolic effect of its own that is covered by the therapeutic uses set out
Halozyme in the MA.
ECJ Forsgren A combination of two substances–where one is an active ingredient with
C-631/13 therapeutic effects of its own and the other is an adjuvant–shall not be
15/01/2015 considered a “combination of active ingredients” even though the
adjuvant makes it possible to increase these therapeutic effects.
ECJ GSK
C-210/13 In the more specific case of safeners, it is necessary to verify whether the
14/11/2013 substance in question has a “toxic, phytotoxic or plant protection action
ECJ Bayer Crop of its own”.
Science AG
C-11/13
19/06/14
5 / INPI / SUPPLEMENTARY PROTECTION CERTIFICATES - SPC / Last change to this page: December 2023

c) The first MA
The MA must be the first authorisation granted in France to place the
product on the market as a medicinal or plant protection product.
Therefore, it cannot be a renewal or a confirmation of an MA granted
previously.
1. Origin of the MA
With respect to medicinal products, the MA is granted by the French
Agency for Veterinary Medicinal Products (Agence nationale du
médicament à usage vétérinaire – ANMV), the French Agency for
Medicinal and Health Product Safety (Agence nationale de sécurité du
médicament et des produits de santé – ANSM) or the European Medicines
Agency (EMA).
With respect to plant protection products, the MA is granted by the
French Ministry of Agriculture.
2. Date of the MA
The French Industrial Property Office (Institut national de la propriété
Art. 3 (b) of
industrielle – hereinafter “INPI”), together with the various medicinal
Reg.
product bodies and the French Ministry of Agriculture, verifies that the
469/2009
MA indicated on the request for the grant of a certificate is the first MA
Art. 3 (1. b) of
granted for the product in France.
Reg. 1610/96
The “date” of a French MA is the date on which the authorisation was
granted: in accordance with French administrative law, a decision
favourable to the addressee–in particular a decision granting the
addressee certain rights–is enforceable upon signature without requiring
notification.
ECJ Seattle
Regarding MAs granted by the European Union, the “date” of an MA is
Genetics
defined by EU law. It can be defined as the date on which the applicant
C-471/14
was given notification of the decision granting the MA.
06/10/2015
3. Subject-matter of the MA
ECJ Medeva An MA must be granted for placing a product on the market as a
C-322/10 medicinal or plant protection product. The fact that the MA also covers
Georgetown other active ingredients than those for which the SPC is requested shall
C-422/10 not prevent the latter from being granted.
24/11/2011
Should several MAs be granted on the same day, it is up to the applicant
to choose which authorisation shall feature on their request: only one
MA may be indicated on the request for the grant of a certificate.
ECJ Santen The MA must be the one first granted for the product subject to the SPC.
C-673/18 Accordingly, the MA for a new medical use of an active ingredient, or
09/07/2020 combination of active ingredients, for which an MA has already been
granted for another medical use, cannot be regarded as the first MA for
the product.
There is no need to take into account the fact that the most recent MA
is the first MA within the scope of the basic patent relied upon for the
purposes of the SPC application.
6 / INPI / SUPPLEMENTARY PROTECTION CERTIFICATES - SPC / Last change to this page: December 2023

ECJ Abraxis An MA for a new formulation of an old active ingredient cannot be
C-443/17 regarded as the first MA for the product at issue as a medicinal product
21/03/2019 when this active ingredient has already been covered by an MA.
.
d) Exclusion of dual protection
Art. 3 (c) of
Reg. 469/2009 The product must not already have been the subject of an SPC in the
Art.3 (1. c) of name of the same holder.
Reg. 1610/96
This condition is understood with regard to each co-holder of a patent
Court of on the basis of which an application for an SPC is made for a same
Cassation product, and not with regard to the group of co-holders, which does not
Plenary have the status of a legal person.
session
P 21-13.664 When an application for an SPC is filed by co-holders of a basic patent, a
01/02/2023 new SPC cannot be granted to any co-holder(s) already in possession of
Ono an SPC for the same product.
The review of an SPC request therefore includes a background check.
The check takes into account all SPCs previously granted in France,
ECJ AHP
irrespective of the date. Consequently, for medicinal products, the check
Manufacturing
takes into account certificates delivered under French Law no. 90-510 of
C-482/07
25 January 1990 and SPCs granted under Regulation (EEC) no. 1768/92
03/09/2009
or Regulation (EC) 496/2009.
The check shall be carried out on the product subject to the request, as
defined above.
The INPI shall verify whether the product is already subject to a certificate
and/or that such product is not included in the extended claims in the
case of certificates governed by French law no. 90-510 of 25 January
1990.
An SPC for A, granted on the basis of a patent for which it is the
innovative active ingredient, precludes the grant of an SPC for A+B
ECJ Actavis I
based on the same patent if B is not “protected by the patent as such”,
C-443/12
even if it is referred to under generic terms such as “beta-blocking
12/12/13
compound”, “calcium antagonist”, “diuretic”, “non-steroidal anti-
inflammatory” or "tranquilliser”, etc. The two SPCs would in fact be
considered to be “connected, wholly or in part, with the same product”.
An SPC for A, granted on the basis of a patent for which it is the
ECJ Actavis II
innovative active ingredient, also precludes the grant of an SPC for A+B
C-577/13
based on the same patent, even if A+B is covered by a subsequent
12/03/15
claim, for example following a limitation, since B is not the “subject-
matter of the invention”.
ECJ
However, if product A has already been subject to an SPC on the basis
Georgetown
of a patent for which it is the innovative active ingredient, this SPC shall
University
not preclude the grant of an SPC for A+B, provided A+B constitutes a
C-484/12
totally separate innovation protected by a separate patent.
12/12/2013
Similarly, an SPC granted for A+B shall not preclude the grant of an SPC
for B based on the same patent provided that A+B, as well as B taken
individually, are protected “as such” by that patent.
7 / INPI / SUPPLEMENTARY PROTECTION CERTIFICATES - SPC / Last change to this page: December 2023

e) Time limit for submitting the application for a certificate
Art. 7 (1) of The application for an SPC must be lodged within six months of the date
Reg. 469/2009 on which the first MA for the product was granted in France or on French
Reg. 1610/96 territory.
Art.7 (2) of The date of this MA is determined as outlined in section 1 c) 2. above.
Reg. 469/2009
Reg. 1610/96 In the event that the MA was granted before the basic patent was
granted, the SPC application must be lodged within six months of the
date on which the patent was granted.
If the basic patent is a European patent designating France, the six-
month period will run from the date of grant of the patent by the
European Patent Office (EPO).
2. OBTAINING AN SPC: FORMAL REQUIREMENTS
The application for a certificate shall contain:
Art. 8 of  a request for the grant of a certificate,
Reg. 469/2009  a copy of the first MA granted in France,
Reg. 1610/96
 where applicable, a copy of the publication in the Official Journal of
the MA number obtained outside of France, within the European
Union.
a) The request for the grant of a certificate
 the request shall contain: the first name and surname or the company
name of the applicant(s), as well as their address(es), and where
applicable, the name and address of the representative to whom all
letters must be sent;
 the references of the basic patent, i.e. its origin, the national or
European application number, the publication number, the date of
filing, the date of grant, and, where applicable, the date of filing of
the translation, as well as the title of the invention;
 the references of the first MA granted in France, i.e. the dossier
ECJ Seattle number, the date on which it was granted or the date of notification
Genetics (see section 1 c) 2. above) and the date of the last renewal, the name
C-471/14 of the product–i.e. the international non-proprietary name (INN)–or
06/10/2015 the usual common name or chemical name of the active ingredients
of the medicinal product;
 where applicable, if the MA referred to in France is not the first MA for
the product to be placed on the market as a medicinal or plant
protection product in the European Union, the references of the first
decision, as well as the country in which it was granted; the name of
the product (see above) and the legal provision under which the
authorisation procedure was carried out.
Where applicable, the application shall state that it includes a request
for a paediatric extension of the SPC (see point 5 below).
b) A copy of the first MA granted in France, including in
particular the number and date of the authorisation, the name of
the product, and the summary of its main characteristics.
8 / INPI / SUPPLEMENTARY PROTECTION CERTIFICATES - SPC / Last change to this page: December 2023

c) Where applicable, a copy of the publication in the Official
Journal of the first administrative decision granting MA in
the European Economic Area for the product, as well as the
extracts making it possible to define the qualitative composition of
the active ingredient(s), as well as a translation of such extracts in
French.
Protocol 1 to Since the entry into force on 1 January 1994 of the Porto Agreement on
the Porto the European Economic Area (hereinafter the “EEA”), the territory taken
Agreement of into consideration includes the 27 Member States of the European
2 May 1992 Union, Iceland, Norway and, since 1 May 1995, Liechtenstein.
(L. no. 93-
1274 of Not all of the Member States have a publication procedure for MAs. In
2/12/1993, this instance, it is enough to state the therapeutic indications and the
OJ 3/12/1993 date on which the MA was granted, or, where applicable, the date of
D. no. 94-193 notification of the MA.
of 1/02/1994
in which the Specific case of MAs in Liechtenstein: as no MAs are granted in this
Agreement country, which approves Swiss MAs, the INPI checks the list of Swiss MAs
was published, awaiting approval in Liechtenstein. Only when the MA has been
OJ approved can it be considered the first authorisation within the EEA.
10/02/1994)
d) Appended items
The application for a certificate must be accompanied by:
- the representative's delegation of power, where applicable (unless
he/she is an industrial property attorney or a lawyer);
- proof of payment of the required filing fee.
NB: This fee must not be confused with the renewal fee paid for the
maintenance in force of the basic patent or the SPC itself.
e) Publication of the application for a certificate
Art. 9-2 of SPC applications which meet the requirements laid down in the
Reg. 469/2009 applicable texts are made public in the form of:
Reg. 1610/96  A notification in the French Industrial Property Journal for Patents
(BOPI Brevets);
 an entry in the French Patent Register;
 the availability of the application to the public (see point 7 below).
The INPI can send, at the request of any person and in return for a fee,
a statement showing the annual fees paid for an SPC and the date of
the last payment.
f) Third-party observations
Third-party observations are deemed inadmissible under this procedure
and are duly dismissed. Corresponding documents are not submitted to
the examiner in charge of the application.
9 / INPI / SUPPLEMENTARY PROTECTION CERTIFICATES - SPC / Last change to this page: December 2023

3. GRANT OF THE CERTIFICATE
After the application has been recognised as being in compliance with the
Art. 10 of provisions of the regulation, the SPC is granted, unless the applicant withdraws their
Reg. 469/2009 request.
It the application is not compliant, the applicant can rectify irregularities (e.g. the
name of the product, MA references) at the request of the examiner who will give
them a deadline for their response.
Art. 11-1 of
Reg. 469/2009 The grant of an SPC is entered in the French Patent Register and is notified in the
Reg. 1610/96 BOPI.
This notification states the duration of the protection (expiry date).
a) Legal duration of the certificate
Art. 13 of
The SPC shall take effect “at the end of the lawful term of the basic patent for a
Reg. 469/2009
period equal to the period which elapsed between the date on which the application
for a basic patent was lodged and the date of the first authorisation to place the
Art.8-1d of
Reg. 469/2009 product on the market in the Community, reduced by a period of five years.”
“The duration of the certificate may not exceed five years.”
ECJ C-471/14 “For the purposes of calculating the duration of the certificate, account shall be
Seattle Genetics taken of a provisional first marketing authorization only if it is directly followed by a
06/10/2015 definitive authorization concerning the same product.”
The method used to calculate the date of the first MA in the European Union is
indicated in section 1 c) 2. above.
Art. 13-3 of
Reg. 1610/96 If the first MA in the EU is a national MA granted by a Member State other than
Recital (17) France, the applicant is responsible for providing proof of the date of effect of this
MA under the national law of this State.
In the case of studies with a view to paediatric use, the duration of protection may
be subject to a one-off extension of six months provided the necessary conditions
are fulfilled (see point 5 below – Extension of SPCs).
b) Maintenance in force
Art. 12 of The maintenance in force of the certificate is subject to the payment of annual fees,
Reg. 469/2009 which are due, at the latest, on the last day of the anniversary month of the filing of
the basic patent.
R.617-1 of the The total payment of all annual fees is possible in the year prior to the entry into
IPC effect of the certificate.
Unlike for patents, the amount does not gradually increase.
Art. L.612-19, The required annual fee may be paid within an additional period of six months,
Art. R. 411-17 subject to payment of an additional late payment fee.
of the IPC
Art. L.613-22 of In the event of payment default, the rights attached to the certificate shall be
the IPC revoked by decision of the CEO of the INPI.
Art. L.612-16 of If the holders are able to provide a legitimate reason, they may submit an appeal in
the IPC order to have their rights reinstated within two months of the removal of the cause
of non-compliance and within one year maximum after the expiry of the grace
period. This same period shall apply to the payment of the annual fees.
10 / INPI / SUPPLEMENTARY PROTECTION CERTIFICATES - SPC / Last change to this page: December 2023

4. REJECTION OF THE APPLICATION FOR A CERTIFICATE
Art. 10 -2 and The SPC application shall be rejected if it is not made within the required time frame
10-4 of and in compliance with the formal requirements, after the applicant has submitted
Reg. 469/2009 their observations and, where applicable, altered their request (see point 6 below).
In the absence of a response within the allocated time frame to a notification of
R.617-2 and irregularities or to a draft refusal, the applicant is notified of the refusal. This refusal
R.612-52 of shall have no effect if a request for further proceeding is submitted within a two-
the IPC month period of the notification of the refusal decision.
The rejection of an SPC application may be subject to an appeal before the Paris
Court of Appeal (see section E, 3.2. of the INPI publication La délivrance des brevets
Art. L.411-4 of et des certificats d’utilité [Grant of patents and utility certificates] – available in French
the IPC only).
Art. D.411-19-
1 of the IPC
5. EXTENSION OF SPCS
Art. 36 of Regulation 1901/2006 of 12 December 2006 on medicinal products for paediatric
Reg. 1901/2006 use provides for the possibility of obtaining a six-month extension of SPCs for
medicinal products that have been subject to research with a view to their use in the
paediatric population.
Art. 13-3 of
An SPC may only be extended once.
Reg. 469/2009
a) Content of the application for an extension of the duration
Art. 8 of The application for an extension of the duration of the SPC must include the
Reg. 469/2009 following:
 the application form for an extension of the duration, which must indicate:
- the first name and surname or the company name of the applicant(s), as well
as their address(es), and where applicable, the name and address of the
representative to whom all letters must be sent;
- the number of the SPC or of the related SPC application, the publication number
of the basic patent;
- that the medicinal product subject to the MA is not an orphan medicinal
product and does not benefit from the one-year extension of the marketing
protection period;
 the copy of the EMA or ANSM statement showing that the MA application
complies with an approved and completed paediatric investigation plan (PIP).
This statement must also indicate that the summary of the product
characteristics reflects the results of studies conducted in compliance with said
PIP in each country of the European Union where appropriate.
Art. 8-4 of  proof of payment of the fee payable upon application for the extension of the
Reg. 469/2009 duration of a certificate.
Where applicable, the following items must also be provided:
 the copy of the SPC if it has been granted;
 if the MA application forming the basis of the request was made at a national
level, the copy of the corresponding MAs in all of the other Member States (26)
making it possible to identify the product subject to the SPC. Where applicable, a
translation in French of the information required to examine the application may
be requested by the INPI.
The MAs must cover the same active ingredient but not necessarily the same
medicinal product. They do not necessarily have to cover a medicinal product for
paediatric use.
11 / INPI / SUPPLEMENTARY PROTECTION CERTIFICATES - SPC / Last change to this page: December 2023

b) Time limit for the application for an extension of the duration
Art. 7-4 and 7- The application for an extension of the duration of a certificate already granted shall
5 of Reg. be lodged not later than two years before the expiry of the certificate.
469/2009
However, any irregularities in the application may be rectified within the time limit
Art. 10 -3 and granted by the examiner.
10-6 of Reg.
469/2009
c) Acceptance of the application for an extension of the duration
Art. 10-1 of If the application for the extension of the duration is completed within the given
Reg. 469/2009 time line, the application shall be accepted.
Art. 13-3 of The duration of the SPC will therefore be extended by an additional six months.
Reg. 469/2009
d) Rejection of an application for the extension of the duration
Art. 10-2 and If an application for the extension of the duration does not satisfy the substantive,
4-6 of Reg. formal and deadline conditions, and if it has not been possible to rectify the
469/2009 irregularities contained therein, it shall be rejected by decision of the CEO of the
INPI.
Art. L.411-4 of
the IPC This decision may be subject to an appeal to set the decision aside before the Paris
Art. D.411-19- Court of Appeal (see Part I, section E, 3.2. of the INPI publication La délivrance des
1 of the IPC brevets et des certificats d’utilité [Grant of patents and utility certificates] – available
in French only).
e) Publication
Art. 11-3 of The application for the extension of the duration of the SPC is notified in the BOPI
Reg. 469/2009 and entered in the French Patent Register, as is the rejection or acceptance of such
application.
f) Required fee (6th renewal fee)
Decision no. When the duration of an SPC exceeds 5 years, because of the extension it has been
2020-36 of the granted, the payment of an additional renewal fee is due on the anniversary of the
CEO filing date of the basic patent.
Rates applied
as of 1 April The publication of the acceptance of the application shall state the new expiry date
2020 of the SPC.
12 / INPI / SUPPLEMENTARY PROTECTION CERTIFICATES - SPC / Last change to this page: December 2023

6. IMPLIED REJECTION OF AN APPLICATION FOR A CERTIFICATE OR FOR THE EXTENSION
OF THE DURATION OF THE SPC PURSUANT TO FRENCH DECREE NO. 2015-1436 OF 6
NOVEMBER 2015
Decree no. Under French decree no. 2015-1436 of 6 November 2015, the SPC grant
2015-1436 of procedure is subject to the “silence is deemed refusal” (SDR) principle after a
6 Nov. 2015 period of one year from the filing date of the application. This period shall be
interrupted in the event that the INPI provides notification of an irregularity.
The provisions of this decree shall be applicable to all SPC applications
filed after 12 November 2014.
Consequently, all irregularities relating to the application shall be notified
within a period of one year from the filing date of said application.
If the observations presented do not allow for the objection to be lifted
or if the irregularities are not rectified within the stated time frame, the
SPC application shall be subject to a draft refusal. This draft refusal shall
be notified within a period of one year from the date of the applicant’s
response to the notification of irregularities.
Should the applicant fail to rectify the situation following a draft refusal,
or if the observations made do not allow for the objection to be lifted, a
refusal decision shall be issued. This decision shall be notified within one
year of the date of the applicant’s response to the draft refusal.
Within the context of the examination, the applicant who has been set
a time limit to submit their observations or to rectify their request, may
obtain an extension of this response time on written request. This
extension may not exceed a maximum of four months.
7. ACCESS TO THE ELEMENTS CONSTITUTING THE SPC APPLICATION
As of the date of publication of the SPC application, any person may view
the elements contained in the application dossier at the INPI and may
obtain a copy thereof at their own expense. Viewing the dossier and the
duplication of the documents contained therein shall give rise to a service
fee, which is set by decision of the CEO of the INPI.
These files are available in electronic format and may be viewed free of
charge online via the INPI patent database (www.inpi.fr).
With regard to SPCs, certain elements are not disclosed to the public, in
particular:
 elements containing personal data or data relating to trade secrets, in
particular the name and address of the manufacturer, as well as the
quantitative composition of the excipients mentioned in the MA.
